steps.title: Apply area formula to find the ratio
steps.content:
Right angle at B makes AC the hypotenuse with AB = 24 and AC = 37 (from AB + 13). The area can be expressed two ways:
  • Using legs: $(1/2) \cdot AB \cdot BC$
  • Using hypotenuse and altitude: $(1/2) \cdot AC \cdot BD$
Setting them equal: $AB \cdot BC = AC \cdot BD$.

Solving for the ratio: $\frac{BC}{BD} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{37}{24}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
